key_insight: The paper shows that flat-band materials can be designed from novel, symmetry-compatible crystal skeletons beyond classic kagome/Lieb/pyrochlore motifs, expanding the geometric search space for flat bands.
engineering_relevance: This provides a generative, symmetry-aware pathway for discovering candidate quantum materials with targeted electronic flatness, which is useful for screening strongly correlated and topological materials at scale.
method: Built a continuous geometric representation of crystal sublattices to go beyond discrete named prototypes.
Used a symmetry-constrained generative model (SkeleGen) to place unconventional skeletons on Wyckoff positions and denoise surrounding chemistry.
Screened generated structures for stability and flat-band behavior.
evidence_signal: The pipeline produced 9,352 crystal candidates that passed stability and flatness screening, and high-throughput DFT agreed well with tight-binding spectra of the isolated skeletons.
watch_item: Because the approach relies on generated candidates and screening, experimental synthesis feasibility and real-world stability may still limit how many predicted flat-band materials can be realized.
paper_title: Symmetry-aware generative design of flat-band materials beyond known crystal-net prototypes
